changes.content: * Calls sboxd workspace/create with structured BootstrapConfig via WebSocket.
* Replaces the env-var-based /api/ready bootstrap path. Bootstrap is idempotent,
* so this is safe to call on every sandbox access (new, warm, or reused).
// When a sandbox is reused (not newly provisioned, not from warm pool) and
// Bootstrap the workspace on sboxd. With the feature flag enabled, we call
// a workload is configured, Nimbus skips the sboxd ready call. We call it
// workspace/create with structured BootstrapConfig for all workload-configured
// directly to bootstrap the workspace (git repos, agent templates, plugins,
// sandboxes (new, warm, reused) since bootstrap is idempotent. Without the
// credentials). Errors are propagated because a failed reconciliation means
// flag, we fall back to the legacy /api/ready path for reused sandboxes only.
// the sboxd session will definitely fail.

new_lines: * Generates environment variables for sandbox provisioning
// Only set INITIAL_ALLOWED_ORIGIN if originHost is provided
// Add FILE_KEY_HASH as SHA1 of the file key
* Calls sboxd /api/ready to trigger workspace bootstrap on a reused sandbox.
* When a sandbox is provisioned before the workload config is known, the sboxd
* bootstrap (git repos, agent templates, plugins, credentials) never runs.
* This function directly calls the sboxd ready endpoint to reconcile,
* matching the same protocol Nimbus uses on OnCreate/OnReady.
// nosemgrep: figma.cortex-use-safe-fetch-instead-of-fetch (sboxd URL is an internal sandbox address from Nimbus, not user-controlled; safeFetch SSRF filter would block private IPs)
* Calls sboxd workspace/create with structured BootstrapConfig via WebSocket.
* Replaces the env-var-based /api/ready bootstrap path. Bootstrap is idempotent,
* so this is safe to call on every sandbox access (new, warm, or reused).
logger.info('Bootstrapping workspace via workspace/create', { sboxd_url: sboxdUrl })
reject(new Error('sboxd workspace/create timed out after 30s'))
* Client for interacting with the Foundry controller API
* Ensures a sandbox is provisioned and returns connection info.
const { req, context, routingKey, forceProvision, originHost, provider } = options
// Check if sandbox will be newly provisioned so we can rate limit new sandbox provisioning via antiabuse
logger.info('Antiabuse disabled for new sandbox provisioning via Statsig gate')
logger.warn('Rate limit bypassed for new sandbox provision', err)
// Generate environment variables internally to ensure consistency across all callers
// Default to true since if we're not sure whether a sandbox will be newly provisioned, we should generate all env variables
const rewrittenSboxdUrl = sboxdUrl ? maybeRewriteSandboxUrl(sboxdUrl, context) : undefined
// Bootstrap the workspace on sboxd. With the feature flag enabled, we call
// workspace/create with structured BootstrapConfig for all workload-configured
// sandboxes (new, warm, reused) since bootstrap is idempotent. Without the
// flag, we fall back to the legacy /api/ready path for reused sandboxes only.
* Makes an authenticated request to the foundry controller
const { method, body, context, originHost, includeAuthzToken, signal } = options
// Normalize path to remove dynamic segments (e.g., workspace IDs)
old_lines: * Generates environment variables for sandbox provisioning
// Only set INITIAL_ALLOWED_ORIGIN if originHost is provided
// Add FILE_KEY_HASH as SHA1 of the file key
* Calls sboxd /api/ready to trigger workspace bootstrap on a reused sandbox.
* When a sandbox is provisioned before the workload config is known, the sboxd
* bootstrap (git repos, agent templates, plugins, credentials) never runs.
* This function directly calls the sboxd ready endpoint to reconcile,
* matching the same protocol Nimbus uses on OnCreate/OnReady.
// nosemgrep: figma.cortex-use-safe-fetch-instead-of-fetch (sboxd URL is an internal sandbox address from Nimbus, not user-controlled; safeFetch SSRF filter would block private IPs)
* Client for interacting with the Foundry controller API
* Ensures a sandbox is provisioned and returns connection info.
const { req, context, routingKey, forceProvision, originHost, provider } = options
// Check if sandbox will be newly provisioned so we can rate limit new sandbox provisioning via antiabuse
logger.info('Antiabuse disabled for new sandbox provisioning via Statsig gate')
logger.warn('Rate limit bypassed for new sandbox provision', err)
// Generate environment variables internally to ensure consistency across all callers
// Default to true since if we're not sure whether a sandbox will be newly provisioned, we should generate all env variables
const rewrittenSboxdUrl = sboxdUrl ? maybeRewriteSandboxUrl(sboxdUrl, context) : undefined
// When a sandbox is reused (not newly provisioned, not from warm pool) and
// a workload is configured, Nimbus skips the sboxd ready call. We call it
// directly to bootstrap the workspace (git repos, agent templates, plugins,
// credentials). Errors are propagated because a failed reconciliation means
// the sboxd session will definitely fail.
* Makes an authenticated request to the foundry controller
const { method, body, context, originHost, includeAuthzToken, signal } = options
